checks.finding: design.md ## Architecture defines the registry, versioned schema, POSIX/PowerShell runtime adapters, and repository/CI/gate callers; it describes their interaction with the existing review-contract foundation and identifies JSON, shell, PowerShell, and CI integration choices for the applicable CLI/infrastructure layers.
SKIP: no explicit component dependency declarations found; checked for circular import patterns in architecture narrative.

[LEGACY COMPAT] Field ## Data Plan absent; legacy design.md predates this template requirement. The existing design nevertheless identifies the registry and versioned JSON schema as its persistent data artifacts.
[LEGACY COMPAT] Field ## API / Contract Plan absent; legacy design.md predates this template requirement. No endpoint, RPC, or event interface is introduced in the requirements or design.

[LEGACY COMPAT] Field ## Security Boundaries absent; legacy design.md predates this template requirement. design.md ## Security considerations still covers fail-closed parsing, non-execution of artifact content, path quoting, traversal and symlink rejection, and bounded legacy exceptions; no PII keywords appear in requirements.md user-facing scope.

[LEGACY COMPAT] Feature Type, ## Frontend Plan, and ## Backend Plan fields are absent; legacy design.md predates these template requirements. The scoped feature is repository CLI/infrastructure enforcement and defines no frontend or user-facing API surface to reconcile.

[LEGACY COMPAT] Field ## Test Strategy absent; legacy design.md predates this template requirement. acceptance-tests.md AC-001 through AC-014 still name concrete shell/PowerShell parity, repository validation, CI, quality-gate, precheck, migration, version, malformed-input, and existing-suite verification outcomes.

All components referenced by design.md are either defined as named artifact paths and integration points in ## Architecture and ## Integration or established by requirements.md and investigation.md, including the review-contract foundation, repository validation, CI, quality gate, and downstream prechecks.

requirements.md ## Constraints requires architecture changes to have an ADR in docs/adr/. design.md ## Architecture and ## Integration introduce a canonical repository-wide workflow-state model and a new shared enforcement integration across repository validation, CI, quality-gate, and review prechecks, but design.md references no ADR. An implementer therefore lacks the required decision record for this architectural departure, and the requirement cannot be verified; this is blocking under the ADR-PRESENT Major contract.
